sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: McDonald's brings back an iconic item from the 1980s
feed.summary: McDonald's is reintroducing its beloved "Changeables" Happy Meal toys from the 1980s and 90s, with 16 updated robot and dinosaur designs. This move is part of the fast-food giant's broader strategy to leverage nostalgia, technology, and loyalty initiatives to attract customers amid economic pressures and increased competition, following similar value-focused offerings like the Extra Value Meals.

feed.title: Ellington Financial launches 8.7 million share offering to redeem preferred stock
feed.summary: Ellington Financial Inc. announced a public offering of 8.775 million shares of common stock to redeem its Series A Preferred Stock. The company, currently trading near its 52-week high, plans to use remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes and potential acquisitions, highlighting its consistent dividend payments and recent financial performance. Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs are joint book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: Assessing Ventas (VTR) Valuation As Earnings Growth And Cash Flow Support An Upward Share Price Trend
feed.summary: Ventas (VTR) is gaining investor interest due to strong earnings growth, consistent revenue, and expanded free cash flow, which align with an upward share price trend. The article indicates a fair value of $85.65, suggesting Ventas is currently undervalued at its trading price of $76.28, despite its P/S ratio being higher than the industry average. However, potential risks like reliance on acquisitions and labor costs could challenge this optimistic outlook.

feed.title: Why Vertex (VRTX) Is Up 8.0% After Analyst Upgrades Spotlight Its CF Strength And Kidney Pipeline
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) recently saw its stock rise by 8.0% following a series of analyst upgrades. These upgrades highlighted the continued strength of its cystic fibrosis portfolio and the promising potential of its kidney disease pipeline, particularly povetacicept's FDA orphan drug status. While the core investment narrative remains its high-margin CF franchise funding rare-disease and gene-editing platforms, the new enthusiasm around povetacicept adds a significant kidney-disease pillar to its growth story, with upcoming Q4 2025 results on February 12th serving as a near-term catalyst.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Misses Q4 Estimates, Shares Surge As Q1 Guidance Shines
feed.summary: Texas Instruments reported its fourth-quarter financial results, missing revenue and earnings estimates. Despite the Q4 miss, shares surged in after-hours trading due to strong first-quarter guidance that exceeded analyst expectations. The company provided an optimistic outlook for Q1 revenue and earnings per share, driving investor confidence.

feed.title: Texas Instruments perks up amid Q4 results, guidance (TXN:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) shares rose 5.5% in extended trading after reporting fiscal fourth-quarter results and guidance that largely met Wall Street's expectations. The analog semiconductor giant announced earnings of $1.27 per share for the period ending December 31.

feed.title: HP Inc. Declares Dividend
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE: HPQ) has declared a cash dividend of $0.3000 per share on its common stock, marking the second dividend of fiscal year 2026. This dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 11, 2026. HP Inc. is a global technology leader focusing on AI-powered devices, software, services, and subscriptions, with approximately 0.9 billion shares outstanding.

feed.title: Citi’s HR department ‘spearheaded’ a harassment campaign, former managing director alleges
feed.summary: A former managing director at Citigroup has filed a lawsuit alleging that the company's HR department spearheaded a "harassment campaign" against her, including "baseless" investigations that ruined her reputation and led to her constructive discharge. The lawsuit claims a "deeply misogynistic" culture at the firm and on Wall Street, enabled by forced arbitration until the 2022 Ending Forced Arbitration of Sexual Assault and Sexual Harassment Act. Citi has denied the allegations, stating they have "absolutely no merit."

feed.title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Declares $0.3000 Quarterly Dividend; 6.2% Yield
feed.summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.3000 per share, leading to an annualized dividend of $1.2 and an annual yield of 6.2 percent. The dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to stockholders of record by March 11, 2026, with the ex-dividend date being March 10, 2026. This announcement provides shareholders with details on the company's dividend payout.

feed.title: Westlake Chemical Partners LP Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Distribution
feed.summary: Westlake Chemical Partners LP (NYSE:WLKP) has declared a quarterly distribution of $0.4714 per unit for the fourth quarter of 2025. This marks the 46th distribution since the Partnership's IPO, payable on February 23, 2026, to unitholders of record on February 6, 2026. The announcement also clarifies that 100% of distributions to non-U.S. investors will be treated as income effectively connected with a U.S. trade or business, making them subject to federal income tax withholding.

feed.title: Non-U.S. holders see maximum withholding on Westlake payout
feed.summary: Westlake Chemical Partners LP announced a fourth-quarter 2025 distribution of $0.4714 per unit, payable on February 23, 2026. Non-U.S. investors will have 100% of their distributions treated as income effectively connected with a U.S. trade or business, subjecting them to the highest applicable federal income tax withholding rate. This distribution marks the 46th quarterly payout since the Partnership's initial public offering.

feed.title: BlackRock Elects Gregg Lemkau to Board of Directors
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has elected Gregg R. Lemkau, Co-Chief Executive Officer of BDT & MSD Partners, to its Board of Directors as an independent director. Laurence D. Fink, Chairman and CEO of BlackRock, highlighted Lemkau's extensive financial, investment, and capital markets experience as invaluable for the firm's future growth. With this election, BlackRock's Board now comprises 19 members, including 16 independent directors.

feed.title: HP to pay $0.30 a share dividend on 0.9B shares this April
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE: HPQ) has declared a cash dividend of $0.30 per share, which is the second dividend for its fiscal year 2026. The dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 11, 2026. With approximately 0.9 billion shares outstanding, the total cash payout is estimated to be about $270 million.

feed.title: Eversource Energy Increases Common Dividend
feed.summary: Eversource Energy's Board of Trustees approved a quarterly dividend increase to $0.7875 per share, payable on March 31, 2026. This announcement highlights Eversource's standing as a national leader in sustainability and corporate citizenship, serving approximately 4.6 million customers across Connecticut, Massachusetts, and New Hampshire. The company continues to focus on empowering a clean energy future through various initiatives.

feed.title: New 40,000-lb Pullmaster winch targets fishing and towing fleets
feed.summary: Dover's TWG division has launched the new Pullmaster 40 M/H Planetary Winch, offering a 40,000-pound line pull. This new model fills a capacity gap between their 25,000-pound and 50,000-pound winches and is designed for rigorous marine, industrial, recovery, energy, and natural resources applications. The product emphasizes Pullmaster's engineering reliability and serviceability while introducing differentiating features.

feed.title: Inside Raymond James’ Rai: AI agent writes 3.2M lines of code a month
feed.summary: Raymond James (NYSE: RJF) has launched Rai, a proprietary digital AI operations agent, to enhance operational decision-making using natural language processing and generative AI. Rai provides curated, personalized answers and is designed with human-in-the-loop oversight. Following a successful pilot, Rai will roll out to specific business units, building on the firm's $1.1 billion annual technology investment and existing high AI user adoption.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NASDAQ:KMB) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $1.28
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NASDAQ:KMB) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.28 per share, a 1.6% increase from its previous dividend. The company boasts a 54-year streak of dividend increases and expects to cover its annual dividend with a 64.0% future payout ratio. Analysts have mixed ratings on the stock, with a consensus "Hold" and an average price target of $118.33.

feed.title: Valero Energy vs. Phillips 66: Which Refining Stock Should You Bet On?
feed.summary: This article compares Valero Energy (VLO) and Phillips 66 (PSX), two major refining stocks, to determine which presents a better investment opportunity. While both benefit from lower crude oil prices, Phillips 66's diversified business model, including midstream and chemicals, offers greater stability across market cycles, justifying its higher current valuation despite both stocks holding a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). Valero, conversely, offers higher sensitivity to refining margins and is suited for investors comfortable with more volatility.

feed.title: Why Brown & Brown (BRO) Shares Are Falling Today
feed.summary: Shares of Brown & Brown (BRO) fell 7.3% after the company reported mixed fourth-quarter 2025 results. While adjusted earnings beat expectations, total revenues of $1.61 billion fell short of analysts' forecasts of $1.64 billion, causing investors to react negatively to the revenue miss. The article suggests this decline, though significant, might not fundamentally alter the perception of the business given its historical volatility.

feed.title: U.S. Markets Closed Mixed Tuesday As Corning Led, Humana Lagged
feed.summary: U.S. stock indexes closed mixed on Tuesday, with some sectors gaining and others declining. Corning (GLW) led the market, experiencing growth, while Humana (HUM) lagged. This article provides a general overview of market performance for the day.

feed.title: F5 Networks (FFIV) Tops Q1 EPS by 80c, Offers Guidance
feed.summary: F5 Networks (FFIV) announced strong Q1 results, reporting an EPS of $4.45 which beat analyst estimates by $0.80, and revenue of $822 million, exceeding the consensus of $755.96 million. The company also provided optimistic guidance for Q2 2026 and the full fiscal year 2026, with EPS and revenue forecasts surpassing current analyst expectations.

feed.title: Prediction: Super Micro Could Join the $1 Trillion Club by 2035
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) is predicted to potentially reach a $1 trillion market capitalization by 2035, despite current slowing growth. The forecast is driven by its expanding role in AI infrastructure and edge computing, new partnerships, and bullish analyst targets. The article suggests that the stock is poised for a breakout.

feed.title: A$37.60 pre-market for ResMed (RMD.AX): Jan 29 earnings to shape guidance
feed.summary: ResMed (RMD.AX) is trading at A$37.60 in pre-market ahead of its January 29, 2026 earnings release, with investors focusing on mask resupply, device demand, and software subscription momentum. Analysts expect Q2 EPS of US$2.68 and revenue near US$1.39 billion, with Meyka AI projecting a 12-month target of A$44.22, suggesting an upside of 17.61%. Key risks include weaker resupply, regulatory setbacks, and currency pressures, while guidance on subscription revenue and mask resupply are expected to drive immediate price movements.

feed.title: Briefly: NCPA, Henry Schein Announce New Executive Leaders
feed.summary: The National Community Pharmacists Association (NCPA) has appointed Casey McAllister as its new Vice President of Finance, bringing over 30 years of nonprofit financial management experience. Concurrently, healthcare solutions provider Henry Schein Inc. (Nasdaq: HSIC) announced Frederick M. Lowery will become its new CEO on March 2, succeeding Stanley M. Bergman, who will remain chairman. Lowery's appointment aligns with Henry Schein's focus on growth and recent acquisitions in the home medical equipment sector.

feed.title: Analog chipmaker Texas Instruments forecasts first-quarter revenue above estimates
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TI) forecast first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, signaling a recovery in demand for its analog chips after a prolonged market slump. The company's shares rose nearly 5% in extended trading, having already increased over 13% this year. Despite missing fourth-quarter revenue estimates, investors are hopeful for an easing of the analog supply glut that resulted from excessive buying during the pandemic.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Reports Fiscal Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc announced strong fiscal second-quarter 2026 results, exceeding expectations with record gross margin, operating margin, and non-GAAP EPS. The company reported revenue of $2.83 billion and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $3.11, driven by strong operational execution and data center demand. Seagate also declared a cash dividend of $0.74 per share and provided a positive business outlook for the fiscal third quarter 2026, projecting revenue of $2.90 billion and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $3.40.

feed.title: Gilead Sciences to Release Fourth Quarter & Full Year 2025 Financial Results on Tuesday, February 10, 2026
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences, Inc. announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results and guidance on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, after the market closes. The company's management will host a webcast at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time on the same day to discuss the results and provide a business update, which will be accessible via their website's Investors section. Gilead Sciences is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing medicines for life-threatening diseases.

feed.title: F5 Reports Strong First Quarter Results with 7% Revenue Growth Including 11% Product Growth
feed.summary: F5, Inc. announced strong financial results for its first quarter fiscal year 2026, with total revenue reaching $822 million, a 7% increase year-over-year. This growth was primarily driven by an 11% increase in product revenue, including a significant 37% growth in systems revenue. The company also raised its fiscal year 2026 outlook, projecting revenue growth of 5% to 6% and non-GAAP earnings per share in the range of $15.65 to $16.05.

feed.title: F5 rides 37% systems surge to boost 2026 revenue and earnings guidance
feed.summary: F5 (NASDAQ: FFIV) announced strong first-quarter fiscal year 2026 results, with revenue rising 7% to $822 million, driven by an 11% product revenue growth, including a 37% surge in systems revenue. Following this performance, the company raised its fiscal year 2026 outlook, projecting revenue growth of 5% to 6% and non-GAAP earnings per share in the range of $15.65 to $16.05. F5 credits its success to alignment with market demands such as hybrid multicloud architectures and enterprise AI adoption, marking its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit product revenue growth.

feed.title: Edison international stock hits 52-week high at 62.62 USD
feed.summary: Edison International's stock reached a 52-week high of $62.62, representing an 11.5% increase over the past year. This milestone follows strong Q3 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations, a 6% increase in its quarterly dividend, and a reiteration of a Buy rating from UBS with a raised price target of $70.00. The company is also pursuing securitization for Woolsey fire claims to strengthen its financial position.

feed.title: TI reports Q4 2025 and 2025 financial results and shareholder returns
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TI) reported its Q4 2025 and full-year 2025 financial results, with Q4 revenue at $4.42 billion and net income of $1.16 billion. For the full year 2025, revenue reached $17.68 billion and net income was $5.001 billion. The company also provided a Q1 2026 outlook, expecting revenue between $4.32 billion and $4.68 billion.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp to Participate in the BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp (Nasdaq: FITB) announced its participation in the BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on February 10, 2026, at approximately 2:40 PM ET. Jamie Leonard and Brennen Willingham will represent the company. An audio webcast and presentation slides will be available live and for 14 days after the conference through the Investor Relations section of www.53.com.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for PepsiCo
feed.summary: This article analyzes PepsiCo's (NASDAQ: PEP) P/E ratio, noting its current share price at $148.34, a 1.70% increase in the current session, and a 2.90% spike over the past month. It highlights that PepsiCo's P/E ratio of 27.73 is lower than the Beverages industry's aggregate of 69.14, suggesting potential undervaluation or lower growth expectations compared to peers. The article concludes by emphasizing that while the P/E ratio is a useful metric, it should be used in conjunction with other financial and qualitative analyses for informed investment decisions.

feed.title: AG James secures over $500K from Dollar Tree for selling lead-contaminated food
feed.summary: New York Attorney General Letitia James has secured nearly $560,000 from Dollar Tree Stores Inc. after an investigation revealed the chain sold lead-contaminated children's food despite receiving a recall notification. The settlement funds will support lead poisoning prevention and response efforts, and Dollar Tree must overhaul its recall policies to ensure dangerous products are removed promptly from shelves. Dozens of children reportedly suffered elevated blood lead levels linked to the recalled WanaBana-brand cinnamon applesauce pouches.

feed.title: Salesforce gives stock to Apromore, Spindle AI and Informatica staff
feed.summary: Salesforce has granted a total of 139,574 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 31 employees from Apromore, 8 from Spindle AI, and 3 from Informatica, following their respective acquisitions. These equity awards were issued under Salesforce's 2014 Inducement Equity Incentive Plan and are subject to a four-year vesting schedule, with initial vesting dates varying by acquisition. The grants aim to incentivize and retain non-executive employees from the newly acquired entities.

feed.title: NextEra Energy weighing nuclear power expansion to deliver to data centers, CEO says (NEE:NYSE)
feed.summary: NextEra Energy is considering expanding its nuclear power fleet to supply electricity to data centers, according to its CEO. The company's stock rose 2.1% after the announcement, putting it on track for its highest closing price in two years. This move highlights the growing demand for reliable power sources for energy-intensive data centers.

feed.title: Suspects in alleged Home Depot theft scheme arrested in Grafton County
feed.summary: Four individuals have been arrested in Grafton County, New Hampshire, following an investigation into an alleged organized retail crime enterprise targeting Home Depot in Littleton. The suspects—Keith Gillilan, Ryan Gauthier, Crystal Pilotte, and Ryan Mooney—are accused of conspiring to steal thousands of dollars worth of merchandise between November and December 2025. All four face charges of organized retail crime enterprise, with three also charged with willful concealment.

feed.title: Fund-Raising and Dealmaking Will Dominate Private Asset Managers’ Earnings
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley analysts will be focusing on fund-raising and dealmaking as major private asset managers begin reporting their year-end earnings. The private-equity firms, such as KKR, Blackstone, and Apollo, are increasingly reliant on their ability to raise capital. This indicates a significant trend in the private asset management industry.

feed.title: Starbucks removes US$250,000-a-year cap for CEO’s private jet use
feed.summary: Starbucks has removed the US$250,000-a-year cap on CEO Brian Niccol's private jet use, mandating that he use the company's private jet for all travel, including personal trips. This decision follows a security review that identified credible threats and heightened media attention in the wake of another executive's shooting. Niccol will no longer reimburse the company for personal jet usage, and his total security costs for 2025 were US$1.1 million, with jet use alone costing nearly US$1 million.

feed.title: Fastenal director Satterlee sells $705k in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Fastenal Co (NASDAQ:FAST) director Scott Satterlee sold 15,964 shares of common stock for $705,453 and exercised options for an equivalent number of shares. This transaction follows Fastenal's fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report, which met EPS expectations but missed revenue targets, leading to varied analyst ratings.

feed.title: Valero Energy vs. Phillips 66: Which Refining Stock Should You Bet On?
feed.summary: This article compares Valero Energy (VLO) and Phillips 66 (PSX), two major refining companies, to determine which is a better investment. While VLO has seen higher price gains over the past year, PSX's diversified business model, including midstream and chemicals, offers greater stability. The article suggests that while both benefit from weak crude prices, Phillips 66's diversification and higher valuation reflect investor preference for stability over Valero's higher sensitivity to refining margins.

feed.title: Enbridge, ONEOK downgraded at J.P. Morgan on growth concerns (ENB:NYSE)
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan downgraded Enbridge (ENB) and ONEOK (OKE) to Neutral from Overweight, citing growth concerns. The downgrade set price targets of C$69 for Enbridge and US$83 for ONEOK. Both stocks experienced slight fluctuations in trading following the announcement.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess Amgen (AMGN) After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
feed.summary: Amgen (AMGN) has seen significant share price gains over multiple years, prompting a reassessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 44.9%, while its P/E ratio is close to its Fair Ratio, indicating it is priced about right based on earnings. The article encourages investors to analyze the stock through "Narratives" to connect personal views with financial forecasts for a more comprehensive valuation.

feed.title: Does Trinity Capital’s Venture Debt Focus Secure TRIN’s High Yield or Stretch Its Dividend Model?
feed.summary: Trinity Capital Inc. is a leading provider of venture debt and equipment financing known for its double-digit dividend yield. The article discusses how the company's investment narrative is tied to the resilience of its venture debt portfolio and the reliability of its dividend, rather than significant valuation rerating. Investors should focus on credit performance, dividend coverage, and the effective use of a new KeyBank facility, while also considering income risk.

feed.title: Urban Outfitters Record Quarter Sparks Fresh Questions On Valuation And Risks
feed.summary: Urban Outfitters reported record quarterly revenue, profits, and earnings per share, surpassing analyst expectations and outperforming many peers in the retail sector. This strong performance, including 12.3% year-over-year revenue growth and 48.2% earnings growth, suggests the company's business model and merchandising strategy are resilient, despite some insider selling. Investors will now monitor the sustainability of this momentum, pricing strategies, and future insider activity as the company's valuation is re-evaluated.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern-served site in Alabama earns platinum REDI designation – Company Announcement
feed.summary: A Norfolk Southern rail-served industrial development site in the Shoals region of northwest Alabama has achieved a platinum designation from the national REDI Sites Program. This recognition signifies the site's readiness for fast-track development, featuring rail access, utility infrastructure, and a skilled workforce, positioning it for industrial and logistics growth in the Tennessee Valley. The designation highlights Norfolk Southern's commitment to developing shovel-ready sites and reinforces the Shoals region's appeal for business and industry.

feed.title: McDonald’s seen delivering inline fourth quarter results as share gains support sales
feed.summary: Jefferies analysts expect McDonald's to report fourth-quarter results consistent with Wall Street estimates, driven by continued market share gains in the US. They project around 4.5% US same-store sales growth and an EPS near $3 for the quarter. Looking ahead, Jefferies maintains a 'Buy' rating and a $360 price target for McDonald's, anticipating ongoing share gains, accelerated unit growth, and robust free cash flow generation.

feed.title: Akamai Is Recognized as a 2026 Gartner® Peer Insights Customers' Choice for Network Security Microsegmentation
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies, Inc. has been distinguished as a "Customers' Choice" in the January 2026 Gartner Peer Insights "Voice of the Customer" for Network Security Microsegmentation, achieving a 99% recommendation rate. This recognition highlights Akamai's strong customer focus and indicates the growing importance of microsegmentation in security strategies. The report, which is in its first year for this category, signifies the market's maturity in recognizing microsegmentation as an essential control.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley’s Anthea Tjuanakis Cox: Longevity and Multigenerational Planning
feed.summary: Anthea Tjuanakis Cox, head of financial planning at Morgan Stanley, discusses the increasing complexity of financial planning, particularly concerning longevity and multigenerational wealth. She highlights that the traditional retirement model is becoming outdated and advises financial advisors to adopt a scenario planning approach for their clients. The article emphasizes the need for comprehensive planning that considers longer lifespans and the involvement of multiple family generations.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Pilots In-Store Wellness Spaces as Retailers Embrace Category
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty is launching "Wellness by Ulta Beauty," a new store-within-a-store concept piloting in four locations. These spaces, ranging from 300-600 square feet, will offer interactive education, wellness advisors, and an expanded product assortment including brands by Naomi Watts and Christina Aguilera. This initiative follows CEO Kecia Steelman's previous indication that wellness could become a billion-dollar business for the company, aligning with the growing trend of "wellness-integrated beauty" in the retail sector.
